mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is an operator that synchronizes Kubernetes Services and Ingresses with DNS providers, allowing dynamic DNS management through Kubernetes resources. It is useful when you want to automate DNS record updates based on your Kubernetes resources.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS using Helm
steps.description:
Add the ExternalDNS Helm repository and install the operator. Ensure you are using Helm version 3.0 or higher.

```bash
helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public --set aws.accessKey=YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY --set aws.secretKey=YOUR_AWS_SECRET_KEY
```

**Note:** Replace `YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY` and `YOUR_AWS_SECRET_KEY` with your actual AWS credentials. Ensure the IAM role has permissions to manage Route 53 records.

steps.title: Configure ExternalDNS for your domain
steps.description:
Create a ConfigMap to specify the domain filter and other settings. This example assumes you want to manage DNS records for the domain `example.com`.

```bash
kubectl create configmap external-dns-config --from-literal=domain-filter=example.com --namespace kube-system
```

Then, update the ExternalDNS deployment to use this ConfigMap:

```bash
kubectl patch deployment external-dns -n kube-system --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/env/-", "value": {"name": "EXTERNAL_DNS_DOMAIN_FILTER", "valueFrom": {"configMapKeyRef": {"name": "external-dns-config", "key": "domain-filter"}}}}]'
```

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS installation
steps.description:
Check if ExternalDNS is running correctly and managing DNS records.

```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system
kubectl logs -f deployment/external-dns -n kube-system
kubectl get services --all-namespaces
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
Remove the ExternalDNS Helm release and clean up any associated resources.

```bash
helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system
kubectl delete configmap external-dns-config --namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS to v0.15
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your existing configuration and resources. Check the release notes for breaking changes. To upgrade:

```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public --set aws.accessKey=YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY --set aws.secretKey=YOUR_AWS_SECRET_KEY
```

**Backup:**
```bash
kubectl get all -n kube-system > external-dns-backup.yaml
```

**Rollback:** If the upgrade fails, you can rollback to the previous version using:
```bash
helm rollback external-dns --namespace kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS not updating DNS records
troubleshooting.description:
If DNS records are not being updated, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs deployment/external-dns -n kube-system
```
Ensure that the IAM role has the correct permissions for Route 53. Also, verify that the domain filter is set correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the ExternalDNS pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the root cause:
```bash
kubectl logs deployment/external-dns -n kube-system
```
Common issues include misconfigured AWS credentials or incorrect domain filters.

troubleshooting.title: Service not found in DNS
troubleshooting.description:
If a service is not found in DNS, ensure that the service is of type LoadBalancer and that the ExternalDNS is configured to watch the correct namespace. Check the service annotations for ExternalDNS:
```bash
kubectl describe service <service-name> -n <namespace>
```

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for additional DNS providers and improved logging.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old configuration flags for AWS provider.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes API versions and enhanced error handling.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of ExternalDNS will have the operator running in the kube-system namespace, dynamically managing DNS records for your specified domain based on Kubernetes resources. You should see the DNS records in your DNS provider reflecting the services and ingresses defined in your cluster.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public --set aws.accessKey=YOUR_AWS_ACCESS_KEY --set aws.secretKey=YOUR_AWS_SECRET_KEY

kubectl get pods -n kube-system
kubectl logs -f deployment/external-dns -n kube-system
kubectl get services --all-namespaces

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl and Helm installed, and AWS CLI configured with appropriate permissions.
